persona: Bystander to overdose, emergency
query: someone just overdosed in front of me what do i do
reply:
**Call 911 immediately if you haven't already.**

If the person is unconscious, not breathing, or having a seizure, emergency services are your first priority.

**If you have Narcan (naloxone) and know how to use it, administer it while waiting for help.**

After calling 911:
- Stay with the person and monitor their breathing
- Place them on their side if possible (recovery position)
- Be ready to tell emergency responders what happened

**You can also call or text 988** (Suicide & Crisis Lifeline) for support and guidance while you wait for help — they're available 24/7.

If you're in the Wilmington area and the person survives, **Coastal Horizons' Quick Response Team (910-833-2052)** engages with overdose survivors to connect them with treatment and support.

**Your safety matters too.** If you're in danger, leave and call 911 from a safe place.

Is the person getting help right now?

persona: Veteran seeking services
query: do you have anything for veterans
reply:
Yes! Coastal Horizons partners with Save a Vet Now (SAVN) to help veterans access outpatient treatment services.

## What We Offer
Through the Veterans Outreach Program, we can help cover co-pays and self-pay costs not covered by insurance, Medicaid, Medicare, or other 3rd party coverage — so cost won't be a barrier to getting care.

## How It Works
- Show proof of veteran status at the front desk (DD-214, DD-215, WD AGO 53/55, NAVMC 78-PD, NAVPERS, or veteran designation on your driver's license)
- Complete a referral form to connect with someone in your county

## Locations
We serve veterans in:
- **New Hanover County**: 615 Shipyard Boulevard, Wilmington — 910-343-0145
- **Brunswick County**: 120 Coastal Horizons Drive, Shallotte — 910-754-4515
- **Pender County**: 803 S. Walker Street, Burgaw — 910-259-0668

## Next Steps
Fill out the veterans referral form here: https://coastalhorizons.org/services/substance-use-mental-health/veteran-services-inquiries-and-referrals/

Or call the main office at 910-343-0145 to get started.

More info: https://coastalhorizons.org/services/veteran-services/

- A copy of your lifetime driving record from the NC DMV (must be signed or stamped by the DMV)

- Payment for the $100 assessment fee

Call **910-259-0668** or email **dwi@coastalhorizons.org**

Coastal Horizons is fully licensed by NC DWI Services and currently offers DWI assessments, group sessions, and individual sessions via telehealth.

If you have other questions, you can also reach the main office at 910-343-0145.

I'm so glad you're reaching out. **Open House Youth Shelter can help.** Yes, all services are **free and confidential** — there is no cost to your teen or your family.

**Call right now:**
- **910-392-7408** (available 24/7)
- Or toll-free: **800-672-2903**

They accept referrals anytime, day or night. You can call as a parent/guardian, and they'll talk through what your teen needs. The shelter is a safe 24-hour facility for youth ages 6–18.

Beyond shelter, they provide:
- Food, clothing, and a safe place to stay
- Counseling services
- Life skills and educational support
- Recreational activities
- Follow-up support for three months after your teen leaves, to help stabilize things at home

**You can also reach out to:**
- **988** (Suicide & Crisis Lifeline) — call or text anytime if you need to talk through what's happening

The people at Open House are trained to help in situations like this. They're ready to listen and work with your family.

Is there anything else I can tell you about how to reach them?

persona: Pregnant 19-year-old facing homelessness
query: im 19 and pregnant and about to lose my housing. is there a program for me?
reply:
Yes — Coastal Horizons has a program for you. The **Open House Transitional and Maternity Housing Program** serves pregnant and parenting young adults ages 16–21 who are homeless or at immediate risk of homelessness.

## What They Offer
- Support finding safe, stable housing
- Monthly rental assistance
- Weekly case management
- Employment and education assistance
- Monthly life skills programming
- Parenting skills and child development support
- General, dental, and mental health service coordination
- 24/7 support and crisis response
- Transportation assistance as needed

## How to Reach Them
**Phone:** 910-515-8146 or 910-622-5553  
**Email:** tbellamy@coastalhorizons.org, gcarbone@coastalhorizons.org, or esjames@coastalhorizons.org

When you call, let them know your situation — they can help you get connected right away. This is a voluntary program, and services continue even after you move into housing.

If you're in crisis or need immediate shelter tonight, you can also call **Open House Emergency Youth Shelter at 800-672-2903 or 910-392-7408** (available 24/7).

- **Outpatient counseling** — individual, group, and family therapy for substance use and mental health

- **Office-Based Buprenorphine** — medication-assisted treatment in an office setting

- **Child First** — infant and young child mental health treatment and prevention
- **Incredible Years** — parenting education and support
- **Intensive In-Home Services** — for children/adolescents with serious emotional, behavioral, or substance use disorders
- **Family Centered Treatment** — home-based family therapy and trauma treatment
- **Day Treatment** — at Rocky Point location

## Crisis Services
- **Rape Crisis Center** — 24/7 crisis line 910-392-7460; office 910-803-2111

## To Get Started
You can fill out an online referral form: https://coastalhorizons.org/services/substance-use-mental-health/new-patient-inquiries-and-referrals/

Or call **910-259-0668** (Pender Substance Use & Mental Health) or the main office at **910-343-0145**.
